etymology.description: phonetic 戶(戶) (hù) + semantic 隹(隹) (zhuī, "short-tailed bird") -> hire
etymology.interpretation: The character 雇(gù, "hire") uses 戶(戶) (hù, "door") phonetically to contribute the sound, while 隹(隹) (zhuī, "short-tailed bird") suggests oversight or vigilance, as in managing or overseeing hired services.

examples.chinese: 公司决定雇一名新员工(公司決定雇一名新員工)。
examples.pinyin: Gōngsī juédìng gù yī míng xīn yuángōng.
examples.english: The company decided to hire a new employee.

examples.chinese: 他打算雇一个私人教练(他打算雇一個私人教練)。
examples.pinyin: Tā dǎsuàn gù yī gè sīrén jiàoliàn.
examples.english: He plans to hire a personal trainer.

examples.chinese: 他们为了项目的进展(他們為了項目的進展)，决定雇几个专家(決定雇幾個專家)。
examples.pinyin: Tāmen wèile xiàngmù de jìnzhǎn, juédìng gù jǐ gè zhuānjiā.
examples.english: To advance the project, they decided to hire several experts.
